title: The Little Duckling Who Found Her Courage
moral: Courage isn't about not being scared — it's about taking a step forward even when your heart is trembling.
moralZh: 勇气不是不害怕，而是即使心在颤抖，也愿意向前迈出一步。

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: On the shores of Sparkling Pond, a fluffy yellow duckling named Daisy was born. She was the smallest of her siblings — but also the quietest.

paragraphs.speaker: Mama Duck
paragraphs.text: "Come on, little ones! Time for your first swimming lesson! Follow me!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The other ducklings chirped happily and waddled after Mama Duck. But Daisy hung back. Her little legs felt wobbly.

paragraphs.speaker: Daisy
paragraphs.text: "Mama... what if I can't swim?"

paragraphs.speaker: Mama Duck
paragraphs.text: "Every duckling feels nervous at first, sweetheart. But you have everything you need inside you. Just take a deep breath and try."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Daisy took a deep breath. She stepped one foot into the water. Then the other. The water was cool and gentle. Soon, she was paddling in circles — wobbly at first, but then smoother and smoother.

paragraphs.speaker: Daisy
paragraphs.text: "I'm doing it! I'm really swimming! Qua— I mean, look at me!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But when Daisy tried to quack with joy, her voice came out as a tiny squeak — not a proper duck quack at all. She tried again.

paragraphs.speaker: Daisy
paragraphs.text: "Qu— quee— quack!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Her siblings stopped swimming and turned around.

paragraphs.speaker: Brother Duck
paragraphs.text: "Did you hear that? She sounds like a squeaky toy!"

paragraphs.speaker: Sister Duck
paragraphs.text: "That's not a quack! That's a QUEEK! Ha ha ha!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The other ducklings burst into giggles. Daisy's cheeks turned pink. She sank lower into the water until only her eyes and beak showed above the surface.

paragraphs.speaker: Mama Duck
paragraphs.text: "That's enough! Everyone's voice is different. Daisy, you quacked just fine. Come on, let's go home."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But Daisy didn't feel fine. All the way home, she stayed quiet. The other ducklings kept quacking loudly and proudly. Daisy just walked behind, head down, not making a sound.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: That night, after everyone had gone to sleep, Daisy slipped out of the nest. She stood by Sparkling Pond under the moonlight. The water was still and silver. There was no one around.

paragraphs.speaker: Daisy
paragraphs.text: "Maybe if I practice enough... I can quack like everyone else."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: She took a big breath and tried. "Quack!" — but it came out as a squeaky "Queek!" She tried again. "QUACK!" — it came out even squeakier. Daisy's shoulders drooped. Her eyes filled with tears.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Just then, a soft voice came from behind the reeds.

paragraphs.speaker: Freddy the Frog
paragraphs.text: "Hey there, little duck. Why are you practicing your quack all alone in the dark?"

paragraphs.speaker: Daisy
paragraphs.text: "Because mine sounds funny. Everyone laughs at me."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Daisy sniffled and told Freddy the whole story. The frog listened quietly, nodding now and then.

paragraphs.speaker: Freddy the Frog
paragraphs.text: "Daisy, do you think my croak sounds nice? Ribbit-ribbit-croak!"

paragraphs.speaker: Daisy
paragraphs.text: "Well... it sounds like a frog. That's how frogs are supposed to sound."

paragraphs.speaker: Freddy the Frog
paragraphs.text: "Exactly! And your quack sounds like a duck — just a different kind of duck. Do you know what I call that squeaky sound of yours?"

paragraphs.speaker: Daisy
paragraphs.text: "A mistake?"

paragraphs.speaker: Freddy the Frog
paragraphs.text: "No! I call it your special song! Every animal has their own voice. The owl hoots, the cricket chirps, the duck quacks — and Daisy squeaks! It's not wrong. It's just yours."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Daisy looked up at the frog. His big round eyes were kind and warm. For the first time, she didn't feel like hiding.

paragraphs.speaker: Daisy
paragraphs.text: "But... what if they still laugh?"

paragraphs.speaker: Freddy the Frog
paragraphs.text: "Then let them laugh! You can't control what others do. But you CAN choose to be brave and be yourself. And being yourself? That takes the most courage of all. Ribbit!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Daisy thought about Freddy's words all the way back to the nest. When she finally climbed in beside her sleeping siblings, she felt something new in her chest — a tiny spark of courage.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The next morning, Mama Duck announced a special singing contest at Sparkling Pond. Every duckling was asked to sing their best song for the whole pond community.

paragraphs.speaker: Sister Duck
paragraphs.text: "Oh! I'll sing first! Listen to me! QUACK-QUACK-QUACK-QUACK!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The sister duck quacked loudly and proudly. The other ducklings went one by one — each quack stronger than the last. The audience clapped and cheered for every duckling.

paragraphs.speaker: Brother Duck
paragraphs.text: "Daisy's turn! Let's hear your famous QUEAK!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Some ducklings started to giggle. Daisy's heart pounded. Her knees trembled. She looked at the crowd and wanted to run away. But then — she spotted Freddy the Frog sitting on a lily pad, nodding at her. She remembered his words.

paragraphs.speaker: Daisy
paragraphs.text: "Courage isn't about not being scared... it's about taking a step forward even when your heart is trembling."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Daisy stepped forward. She closed her eyes. And she let out the loudest, squeakiest, most unusual quack the pond had ever heard.

paragraphs.speaker: Daisy
paragraphs.text: "QUEEEK-QUEEEK-QUEEEK-QUEAK!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The pond fell silent for a second. Daisy opened her eyes, ready to run. But then — a single big "RIBBIT!" came from the lily pad. Freddy was clapping his little frog hands.

paragraphs.speaker: Freddy the Frog
paragraphs.text: "Bravo! That was beautiful! The most unique song I've ever heard!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And then something wonderful happened. One by one, the other animals joined in. A rabbit stomped her foot in applause. A squirrel clapped. Even the fish splashed their tails.

paragraphs.speaker: Mama Duck
paragraphs.text: "Daisy, I am SO proud of you! You were the bravest one of all today!"

paragraphs.speaker: Daisy
paragraphs.text: "Really? Even with my squeaky quack?"

paragraphs.speaker: Mama Duck
paragraphs.text: "Especially because of it! You see, Daisy — being brave doesn't mean being perfect. It means showing up, trying your best, and letting the world hear YOUR voice, whatever it sounds like."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The little duckling who had been so afraid to make a sound was now the most celebrated singer at Sparkling Pond. And from that day on, Daisy never hid her voice again.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Whenever you hear a funny squeak near a pond, listen closely — it might just be Daisy, using her courage to sing her own special song.
